title: TC001-Landing page loads and shows main services overview
description: Verifies the public homepage loads successfully and presents the main services overview content to the user.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert '/' in current_url
        current_title = await frame.evaluate("() => document.title")
        assert 'MedQueue' in current_title
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'MedQueue')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'MedQueue' to be visible"
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'EduMatch')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'EduMatch' to be visible"
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Get Started')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Get Started' to be visible"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Homepage did not render: page shows no interactive elements and appears blank after navigation and waiting.
- Expected visible text 'MedQueue' not found on the page.
- Expected visible text 'EduMatch' not found on the page.
- Expected 'Get Started' element not found on the page.

title: TC002-Get Started navigates from Landing Page to Auth selection
description: Verifies the primary call-to-action on the homepage routes the user to the authentication selection page.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Get Started')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Get Started' to be visible"
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert '/auth' in current_url
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Patient')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Patient' to be visible"
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Admin')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Admin' to be visible"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Homepage returned blank content: 0 interactive elements present on http://localhost:5173
- 'Get Started' primary CTA not found on the homepage
- SPA failed to load / site unavailable at http://localhost:5173
- Cannot navigate to the authentication selection page because no navigational elements are present on the homepage

from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability

title: TC014-Patient can join a queue and see updated queue position

code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():

            headless=True,
            args=[

                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow

        # -> Navigate to /auth/patient (http://localhost:5173/auth/patient) to reach the patient login page.

    finally:

        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173")
        
        # -> Navigate to /auth/admin (required explicit navigation step).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/auth/admin")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Admin')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Admin' to be visible"
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert '/medqueue' in current_url
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Live queue list')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Live queue list' to be visible"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC025-Queue empty state: Call Next is disabled or shows 'no patients' message

code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173")
        
        # -> Navigate to /auth/admin (http://localhost:5173/auth/admin) to access the admin login page.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/auth/admin")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Empty queue')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Empty queue' to be visible"
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Call Next')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Call Next' to be visible"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
